message:
fix(bv): normalize triage directory paths to absolute for consistent caching

Extract normalizeTriageDir() helper that resolves empty dir to cwd and
converts the result to an absolute path via filepath.Abs(). This fixes
two bugs in the triage cache:

1. Cache key consistency: previously, calling GetTriage("") followed by
   GetTriage("/data/projects/ntm") would miss the cache even though they
   refer to the same directory, because the cache compared the raw input
   string ("" vs "/data/projects/ntm"). Now both resolve to the same
   absolute path before comparison.

2. Relative path ambiguity: calling GetTriage("../ntm") from different
   working directories could produce cache hits for the wrong project.
   filepath.Abs() resolves relative paths against cwd, producing a
   canonical absolute path for the cache key.

Both GetTriage (cached) and GetTriageNoCache (fresh, but updates cache)
now use normalizeTriageDir() before any cache lookup or bv command
invocation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
